description:
You are an Industrial Engineer at an automotive parts manufacturer preparing for an upcoming ISO 9001:2015 surveillance audit. Using the QC_ENGINEER schema in Oracle (User: qc_engineer, Password: Quality2024, Host: localhost, Port: 1521, Service: XEPDB1), you must analyze production measurement data to generate an audit-ready summary report.

Create a view named PROCESS_CAPABILITY_VW that calculates the process mean (X-bar), standard deviation (sigma), Cp, Cpk, sample count, and date range for each part and production line. Include a capability_rating column categorized as 'EXCELLENT' (Cpk >= 1.67), 'CAPABLE' (Cpk >= 1.33), 'MARGINAL' (Cpk >= 1.00), or 'INCAPABLE' (Cpk < 1.00).

Implement Western Electric Rules 1 through 4 to detect out-of-control processes. Create a table named CONTROL_VIOLATIONS (including violation_id, part_id, line_id, run_id, rule_number, violation_description, measurement_mean, control_limit, and detection_date) and a PL/SQL procedure named PROC_DETECT_VIOLATIONS to identify and record violations for single points beyond 3 sigma, 2 of 3 points beyond 2 sigma, 4 of 5 points beyond 1 sigma, and 8 consecutive points on one side of the center line. Execute this procedure to populate the table.

Additionally, create a DEFECT_PARETO_VW showing defect types ranked by frequency with a cumulative percentage column, and a CALIBRATION_ALERTS_VW identifying instruments with a next_calibration_date prior to SYSDATE. Finally, create a materialized view QUALITY_AUDIT_MV using ROLLUP to summarize defect rates (rejected_qty / batch_size) by production line, part, and shift, including subtotals at the line and part levels. Export the quality audit summary to /home/ga/quality_audit_report.csv.

embedded_signals.mean_drift: Line 2 Bearing Housing drifts upward simulating tool wear
embedded_signals.mean_shift: Line 1 Brake Rotor shifts suddenly due to material lot change
embedded_signals.increased_variation: Line 3 Valve Spring has standard deviation jump simulating fixture loosening
